rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Petition To Terminate Conservatorship And Of The Final Accounting Due To The Death Of The Conservatee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): Conservator has not filed final report on waiver of account. This matter will be called with PTR-14-298368. Conservator must appear with counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADRIAN M. TYLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the original bank statements were filed conventionally, necessitating additional effort by the clerk to provide them to the Examiner, rather than E-filed along with a declaration signed by the attorney or fiduciary certifying that he/she will retain the original as required by LRSF 2.11 M.4. [LRSF 14.35 D. and 14.60 D.] A proposed order should order only what was prayed, not include findings. In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]," as the case may be. [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: SARA R. JULIAN IRREVOCABLE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF ISAAC JULIAN BOOTS,DATED 1/16/18 EST. PURSUANT TO COURT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Matter set for hearing for the sole purpose of determining why counsel has not filed a response to the Examiner Notes. a. Please provide legal authority for the account beginning 01/17/2019 and ending 02/29/2020. b. Please explain the delay in funding the qualified tuition plan. c. Page 5 line 9 of the petition alleges an ex parte petition for increase of bond will be filed under separate cover. Ex Parte has not been filed. [LRSF 14.14 B.] d. On Schedule A-2 please explain the entry 02/24/2020 Sara Julian Trust Per order dated 12/20/19 $441,182.01. There appears to be no 12/20/19 order. The 12/20/2018 Order authorized $500,000.00 from the Sara R. Julian Trust to the Irrevocable Trust. Please explain why $441,182.01 + $25,000.00 was transferred and not $500,000.00. e. On Schedule A-2 please explain the entry 02/28/2020 Sara Julian Trust Per order dated 12/20/20 $70,000.00. f. In the 10/02/2020 Amendment Exhibit A please explain entry 06/27/2019 Reconciled client accounts for 2.50 hours.

PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AMELIA WAI KAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/10/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUEN MOON LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/2021. 1) Please explain and provide legal authority for why petitioner alleges the Will and is self-proving. The Will does not appear to be self-proving under Prob. Code 8220(b) and CCP 2015.5. Petitioner must prove the Will. 2) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 3) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of bond being issued prior to the proposed Executor being appointed by the court. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE ARMISTEAD CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no courtesy copies were provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiner elected to review these initial filings as a ONE-TIME courtesy. (It is also unclear why an estate is being opened when the petitioner cannot estimate the value of the estate.) Courtesy paper copies of all future filings must be provided in order to avoid an automatic continuance for lack of courtesy copies. Status of closing the estate is set for 4/26/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 4/26/2022. Appearance will be required on 4/26/2022 if no petition has been filed.
